top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 127, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
12,600ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
99ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo Nove Colli's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
Gran Fondo Nove Colli's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 127 miles.
At Gran Fondo Nove Colli, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
12,600ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo Nove Colli. Train it until it feels boring.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 127 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Gran Fondo Nove Colli, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
127 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.

race_overlay.heat: May in Cesenatico, Emilia-Romagna, Italy can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Gran Fondo Nove Colli. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo Nove Colli’s 127 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Gran Fondo Nove Colli. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Gran Fondo Nove Colli requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, VO2max, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 127 miles of mixed terrain in Cesenatico, Emilia-Romagna, Italy.
